raw_consumer_statement:
What happened I am submitting this complaint regarding a denied billing dispute with Citibank involving a transaction with XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ) dated XX/XX/year> in the amount of {$1500.00}. 
Citibank initially issued a conditional credit but later reversed it after determining there was no billing error. I disagree with this conclusion because the charge resulted from material misrepresentation and deceptive sales practices, not a valid, informed authorization. 
During the sales presentation, I repeatedly declined the offer and clearly stated no multiple times. Despite this, the sales representatives continued to pressure me with additional incentives until I ultimately agreed. This created a high-pressure environment that did not allow for a fully informed decision. 
The decision to proceed was based on representations that were misleading or incomplete, including : I was told I would have access to XXXX XXXX  weeks for {$290.00} per week, without disclosure that additional fees would be required to actually use those weeks I was led to believe that the travel rates through the program would be significantly discounted, but in reality, the pricing is not competitive and is often equal to or higher than publicly available rates I was promised a trip to XXXX, which has not been provided Unlimited Experiences were presented as included benefits, without disclosure that additional spending would be required to access them I was told I could travel broadly, including to destinations in XXXX, which was misleading as these options require additional costs and are not included as represented These misrepresentations were material to my decision. Had accurate and complete information been disclosed, I would not have agreed to the transaction. 
This is not a case of buyers remorse. It is a dispute involving deceptive and unfair business practices that resulted in a charge being authorized under misleading circumstances.
